title: Tillandsia paraibica
intro: Tillandsia paraibica is a type of epiphytic bromeliad, a plant that grows on other surfaces without deriving nutrients from them. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Tillandsia paraibica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Tillandsia paraibica include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Tillandsia paraibica is classified within the family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pineapples or air plants. This classification places Tillandsia paraibica within the order Poales, along with other monocots. Further, it is placed within the subfamily Tillandsioideae, a subfamily that contains air plants with adapted leaves for absorbing moisture and nutrients from the air. Tillandsia paraibica is also categorized within the genus Tillandsia, which is a diverse group of epiphytes that thrive in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further distinguished by its unique characteristics and specific distribution.
